title: Head to Feet, Settling
intention: To move attention gently through the body, region by region, releasing tension and returning to the breath.

lines.text: Welcome. Let yourself arrive here, just as you are.
Find a position that feels steady and easy, lying down or sitting.
Let your eyes close, or soften your gaze toward the floor.
There is nothing to fix and nowhere to go.
Take a slow breath in.
And let it go, a little longer than the breath in.
Let the body grow heavy, held by the surface beneath you.
We'll travel through the body, region by region, from head to feet.
Bring your attention to the top of your head.
Notice whatever is here, and soften the scalp.
Let your attention move to your forehead and your eyes.
Let the brow smooth. Let the eyes rest deep in their sockets.
Now the jaw, the tongue, the whole of the face.
Let the teeth part slightly, and let the face be soft.
Move down into the neck and the throat.
If you find tension here, simply note it, and let it pass.
Let your shoulders drop away from your ears.
Follow the arms down, through the elbows, into the hands.
Let the fingers be loose and unhurried.
Return to the breath for a moment. Just a few easy breaths.
Bring your attention to the chest, rising and falling on its own.
And the upper back, resting, supported.
Let your attention settle into the belly.
Let it be soft, moving with each breath.
Move down into the hips and the base of the spine.
Let any holding here gently release.
Follow the legs down, through the thighs and the knees.
Down through the calves, into the ankles.
And finally, the feet, all the way to the toes.
Now sense the whole body at once, resting as one.
If the mind has wandered, that's fine. Note it, and come back to the breath.
Rest here, soft and whole, for a few more breaths.
When you're ready, let your attention widen to the room around you.
Carry this quiet with you, and let it stay.
